creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.
contentSnippet: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

creator: Sean O'Kane
title: (No AI) Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
content: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.
contentSnippet: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

title: (No AI) This could be Windows’ M1 moment — but expect it to cost a ton
author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
content:
![Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang holds up two RTX Spark laptops at Computex 2026](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Screenshot_20260531_212701.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Nvidia's announcement that it's [getting into the consumer laptop chip space](https://www.theverge.com/tech/940589/nvidia-rtx-spark-n1-n1x-laptop-desktop-pc-cpu-gpu-ai-release-date) with [RTX Spark](https://www.theverge.com/gadgets/940794/first-nvidia-rtx-spark-laptops-roundup-computex-2026) is huge. Apple has proved for years that Arm-based chips can perform incredibly well while also delivering great battery life - at least on the Mac. In the Windows world, performance hasn't fully matched up under Qualcomm chips, mostly in the graphics department. There's clearly still untapped potential, and Nvidia seems to be promising to deliver it.

This could be Windows' moment to blow us away with a new generation of supremely capable chips, much like Apple's back in 2020, with the introduction of the M1. But why does this launch feel simultaneous …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/941215/windows-laptops-nvidia-rtx-spark-apple-m1-arm-price-ram)
contentSnippet:
Nvidia's announcement that it's getting into the consumer laptop chip space with RTX Spark is huge. Apple has proved for years that Arm-based chips can perform incredibly well while also delivering great battery life - at least on the Mac. In the Windows world, performance hasn't fully matched up under Qualcomm chips, mostly in the graphics department. There's clearly still untapped potential, and Nvidia seems to be promising to deliver it.
This could be Windows' moment to blow us away with a new generation of supremely capable chips, much like Apple's back in 2020, with the introduction of the M1. But why does this launch feel simultaneous …
Read the full story at The Verge.

In the Windows world, performance hasn't fully matched up under Qualcomm chips, mostly in the […]

title: (No AI) Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo
author: Jay Peters
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/lcimg-0ebbe911-9fbb-4f68-97b0-b7361d7cb53c.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google's new "24/7" AI agent, Gemini Spark, can be shockingly good at doing things on your behalf. But I'm not sure it's worth the financial cost and potential privacy tradeoffs.

The company gave me access to Spark last week. Google advertises Spark as an AI agent that can take on tasks and work on them in the background - even tasks that have multiple steps - allowing you to put your phone down or walk away from your computer. It also advertises [_at the very top_ _of the Spark website_](https://gemini.google/overview/agent/spark/) that it's "always under your direction," that "you choose to turn it on," and that "it's designed to check with you before taking major actions." Given the moun …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/941138/google-gemini-spark-ai-agent-hands-on)

title: (No AI) Building the infrastructure for the Intelligence Age in Michigan
content: OpenAI breaks ground on a 1GW data center project in Michigan as part of Stargate, building AI infrastructure to expand access, create jobs, and support communities.

title: (No AI) Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
content: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to improve patient care, reduce operational burden, and help diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases.
contentSnippet: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to improve patient care, reduce operational burden, and help diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases.

title: (No AI) We’re launching the Google DeepMind Accelerator program in Asia Pacific to tackle environmental risks

creator: Paresh Dave
title: (No AI) Anthropic Confidentially Files for What Could Be the Largest IPO Ever
dc:creator: Paresh Dave
content: The AI giant behind Claude submitted paperwork on Monday that would take it public, just a couple of weeks after SpaceX’s splashy IPO announcement.

creator: Caroline Haskins
title: (No AI) Norse Atlantic Airways Offers Dirt-Cheap Tickets. There’s a Catch
dc:creator: Caroline Haskins
content: Dozens of people have complained to the Federal Trade Commission about Norse Atlantic Airways’ tech-first customer service operation. Some said they lost thousands of dollars.
contentSnippet: Dozens of people have complained to the Federal Trade Commission about Norse Atlantic Airways’ tech-first customer service operation. Some said they lost thousands of dollars.
